title: South Boston woman fatally stabbed boyfriend during an argument, police say
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Gisselle Pascual, 24, of South Boston, pleaded not guilty to manslaughter for allegedly stabbing boyfriend Kyle Bradford, 26, during an argument.
Pascual was held on $100,000 bond after police found Bradford with a fatal stab wound at 258 Old Colony Ave., South Boston.
Pascual's lawyer claims self-defense, citing threats from Bradford, who had a history of dismissed domestic assault charges.

title: Patriots to be featured on HBO’s ‘Hard Knocks’ ahead of 2027 season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The New England Patriots will be featured on HBO's 'Hard Knocks' for the first time in 2027.
The Seattle Seahawks will appear on 'Hard Knocks' for the 2026 season, following their Super Bowl win over the Patriots.
NFL rule changes now allow playoff teams like the Patriots to be featured on 'Hard Knocks' despite past rejections.

title: James Hagens notches first points with Providence Bruins
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens scored his first professional goal in Providence Bruins' 6-4 win over the Bridgeport Islanders.
Hagens, 19, recorded a primary assist in Providence's 4-2 loss to the Springfield Thunderbirds, extending his point streak.
Providence coach Ryan Mougenel praised Hagens' skating and playmaking on a line with Matthew Poitras and Georgii Merkulov.

title: Could Patriots circle back with Stefon Diggs? Here’s what Eliot Wolf said of a potential reunion.
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Patriots released Stefon Diggs due to a $26.8 million cap hit in 2026.
Eliot Wolf stated the Patriots haven't ruled out re-signing Diggs at a reduced cost.
Diggs had 85 catches for 1,013 yards with the Patriots in 2025, besting 1,000 yards for the first time since 2019.

title: Man sentenced for raping 2 women in Framingham store in 1989
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Stephen Paul Gale, 73, sentenced to 50 years in prison for 1989 rapes at a Framingham store.
Gale was convicted of four counts of aggravated rape, two counts of kidnapping, and armed robbery.
Cold case solved using investigative genetic genealogy started in 2022; Gale identified as suspect in 2024.

title: Celtics’ Joe Mazzulla on possibility of winning coach of the year: ‘It’s a stupid award’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Celtics coach Joe Mazzulla dismisses NBA Coach of the Year award, calling it a 'stupid award' and credits his team.
Despite injury setbacks, Celtics achieve fifth-consecutive 50-win season and rank second in the Eastern Conference.
Mazzulla emphasizes focus on player development and team effort over personal accolades in his coaching philosophy.

title: 1 dead after crash in Mattapan, police say
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: One person was killed in a car crash in Mattapan late Sunday night, according to Boston Police.
The crash happened at 11:14 p.m. on American Legion Highway at Walk Hill Street and Canterbury Street.
The victim was found in the car on the sidewalk and pronounced dead at the scene, police reported.

title: Should Joe Mazzulla win Coach of the Year? Here’s how he stacks up against the competition.
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Joe Mazzulla has the second-best odds to win NBA Coach of the Year, following Detroit's J.B. Bickerstaff.
The Celtics lost key talent but remain a strong contender, with a top-five NBA record under Mazzulla's guidance.
Boston has shown development across its roster, excelling in both offensive and defensive metrics this season.

title: Ice sculpture carved for Boston’s ‘No Kings’ rally destroyed by vandals, organizers say
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: A 24-foot 'END ICE' ice sculpture was destroyed by vandals on Boston Common after a 'No Kings' rally.
Artist Kat Dressler designed the sculpture to still convey its message if vandalized, highlighting opposition to ICE.
Boston police were notified, but no report was filed; a sign for Activist Evenings was also vandalized nearby.

title: Worcester man gets 30 years in prison for sexually exploiting minors
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Antonio Merced, 29, was sentenced to 30 years for sexually exploiting three minors, including a 6-year-old girl.
Federal sentence will run concurrently with Merced's 25 to 30-year state sentence for related charges.
He pleaded guilty to possession of child pornography and creating explicit content with minors in July 2025.
